objective: C3A-CP3 implements the next Checkpoint 3A child checkpoint: an executable language-quality probe that executes the local product/API path and evaluates generated runtime walkthrough output quality from stored API-visible output and evidence. Positive claims: C3A-CP3-HARNESS-001 dispatches the language-quality probe as an implemented executable pytest probe with shell=False and bounded/redacted failure output; C3A-CP3-LANGUAGE-001 creates approved synthetic projects through local API behavior, uploads approved synthetic knowledge, ingests/chunks/stores it, generates grounded walkthrough output, retrieves stored output/evidence through API-visible behavior, and verifies coherent walkthrough structure, audience-appropriate tone, citation readability, no placeholder text, no debug/internal leakage, no trivially short output, and no unsupported cross-project language; C3A-CP3-NEG-001 proves docs/prose/static-snapshot or canned success behavior inadequate by rejecting style text without runtime API evidence, grounded-looking placeholder language, trivially short citation output, malformed citation placement, debug/internal leakage, and unsupported cross-project language insertion. Later media artifacts, access/quota/retention, security/observability, performance, and real-browser E2E probes remain planned and do not complete Checkpoint 3A.
